title: Peruvian Tacu Tacu with Salsa Criolla
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no cholesterol-contributing animal-derived ingredients. The beans, rice, onion, garlic, spices, herbs, chile, vinegar, lime juice, and olive oil contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the dish is built around beans, a legume base rich in soluble fiber, with rice, onion, herbs, chile, citrus, vinegar, and a restrained amount of unsaturated oil providing structure, flavor, and satisfaction without cholesterol-containing ingredients.
intro: Tacu tacu is one of Peru's great rice-and-bean comfort dishes, often made by frying leftover rice and beans together until the outside browns and the inside stays soft. It is practical food turned into something better through heat, pressure, and crust. Versions are often served with steak, seafood, egg, or plantain, but the rice-and-bean cake is the heart of the dish. Salsa criolla brings the sharp lime, onion, chile, and cilantro that cut through the richness and make the whole plate feel alive.

items.item: dried canary beans, pinto beans, or black beans

items.prep: rinsed until the water runs mostly clear

items.item: aji amarillo, Fresno chile, or jalapeño

stepGroups.title: Soak the Beans
items: Place the beans ({{quantity:dried-beans}}) in a large bowl and cover with several inches of water.
Soak overnight.
Drain and rinse the beans.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Beans
items: Add the soaked beans, water ({{quantity:water-cooking-beans}}), and bay leaf ({{quantity:bay-leaf}}) to a pot.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Cook for 60 to 90 minutes, until the beans are tender.
Drain the beans and discard the bay leaf.
Mash half of the beans lightly so some stay whole and some become creamy.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Rice
items: Combine the rice ({{quantity:long-grain-white-rice}}), water ({{quantity:water-rice}}), and fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-rice}}) in a saucepan.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce to low, cover, and cook for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the rice stand, covered, for 10 minutes.
Fluff with a fork.

stepGroups.title: Make the Salsa Criolla
items: Combine the red onion ({{quantity:red-onion-salsa}}), lime juice ({{quantity:lime-juice}}), red wine vinegar ({{quantity:red-wine-vinegar}}), chile ({{quantity:fresh-chile}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-salsa}}), and cilantro ({{quantity:fresh-cilantro-salsa}}).
Toss well.
Let stand for at least 15 minutes while the rice and bean mixture is built.

stepGroups.title: Build the Bean-Rice Base
items: Heat the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-base}}) in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced red onion ({{quantity:red-onion-base}}).
Cook for 5 to 6 minutes, until softened.

Add the garlic ({{quantity:garlic}}), cumin ({{quantity:ground-cumin}}), smoked paprika ({{quantity:smoked-paprika}}), fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-base}}), and black pepper ({{quantity:black-pepper}}).
Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cooked beans and rice.
Fold together until evenly combined.
Stir in the cilantro ({{quantity:fresh-cilantro-base}}).
The mixture should be moist enough to hold together when pressed, but not wet. If it is too loose, cook it for 2 to 3 minutes over medium heat to drive off excess moisture.

stepGroups.title: Shape the Cakes
items: Divide the mixture into 4 portions.
Press each portion into an oval or thick patty, about 3/4 inch thick.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Tacu Tacu
items: Heat half of the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-cakes}}) in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add 2 cakes.
Cook for 4 to 5 minutes, until deeply browned and crisp on the first side.
Turn carefully.
Cook for 4 to 5 minutes on the second side.
Transfer to a rack.
Repeat with the remaining cakes.

stepGroups.title: Serve
items: Serve the tacu tacu hot with salsa criolla spooned over the top.
